reasoning: The target threshold is 106.03 (value on May 25). For June 1 (t+7), short-term noise and a possible slight seasonal start-of-month bump (seen in 2024, supported by the tool's 0.52 prior) make an increase plausible but slightly disfavored given the recent 15-day mean of 105.96. For longer term, the labor market trend is structurally downward, dropping from 111 in January to 106 in May (~1 pt/month) driven by federal spending and contracting cuts under the new administration. This strong directional signal gives us more confidence that the index will be lower by June 24 (t+30). For distant dates (August, November), macroeconomic uncertainty dominates—the index could approach the strong support baseline of 100 and stabilize, or rate cuts could spark a rebound. This deep uncertainty for the distant horizons appropriately pushes the probabilities back towards 0.5.
